title: Deterministic Tests, Fault Injection, Contract Evidence, and Observability · Automation and Data Client Sys… · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: A research data operations team. The team needs python client fault and contract harness with protocol-correct behavior, bounded authority, changed-case tests, and an operational defense. Planned artifact: Deliver Python Client Fault and Contract Harness with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inject the narrow request execution capability needed for deterministic tests without cloning the whole HTTP library. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Inject cancellation, timeout, reset, partial body, malformed fields, bad media type, redirect, and retry exhaustion deterministically. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Minimize outbound data and authority and redact credentials, personal data, URLs, and bodies from logs and errors. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Commit to the mission model · Project Transport Abstraction

project case — Python stakeholder client test P-2 injects a delayed header, truncated gzip body, malformed JSON, cross-origin redirect, and cancellation. Competency probe: Record stable client duration, method, route or safe target, status, error type, attempts, bytes, and trace context without high-cardinality secrets. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

For Python Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Http Telemetry, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Logging the complete URL and headers produces ideal HTTP observability. This remains false for Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers.
